claims.claim_text: Opening incident anecdote says a platform team at a large subscription commerce company saw checkout latency rise at 02:18 across three regions while Kubernetes reported healthy Deployments and customers abandoned carts after several seconds of delay.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 1
claims.anchor_text: At 02:18, a platform team at a large subscription commerce company sees checkout latency rise across three regions while Kubernetes still reports every Deployment as healthy. The application pods are running, readiness probes are green, and the load balancer is still accepting traffic, but customers are abandoning carts because confirmation pages take several seconds to load. The incident commander needs a way to compare request rate, error ratio, database latency, and pod restarts across thousands of time series without reading logs from every replica.
claims.suggested_rewrite: During a checkout incident, a platform team can see user-facing latency rise while Kubernetes still reports Deployments as healthy. The application pods may be running, readiness probes may be green, and the load balancer may still accept traffic, but customers can still experience slow confirmation pages. The incident commander needs a way to compare request rate, error ratio, database latency, and pod restarts across many time series without reading logs from every replica.
claims.rationale: The anecdote contains fake-precise incident details without an attributable real source, so the operational lesson should remain while the specifics are removed.

claims.claim_text: Prometheus targets expose measurements over HTTP and the Prometheus server periodically scrapes those targets.
claims.span_hint: section: Prometheus as an Operating Model, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: The Prometheus overview directly documents the pull-based HTTP scrape model.

claims.claim_text: If Prometheus cannot scrape a target, the failure becomes observable through the generated `up` metric.
claims.span_hint: section: Prometheus as an Operating Model, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: The Prometheus jobs and instances documentation defines `up` as the scrape-health series.

claims.claim_text: A Prometheus time series is identified by metric name plus one exact set of labels.
claims.span_hint: section: Prometheus as an Operating Model, paragraph after diagram
claims.rationale: The Prometheus data model documentation directly defines series identity this way.

claims.claim_text: Unbounded labels such as user IDs, request IDs, sessions, raw paths, or exception messages can create many new time series and drive memory pressure and slow queries.
claims.span_hint: section: Prometheus as an Operating Model, paragraph after Stop and think
claims.rationale: The naming guide covers unbounded label cardinality; the storage documentation is a direct additional source for memory implications.

claims.claim_text: Prometheus is a metrics system, not a log database, distributed tracing backend, or general analytics warehouse.
claims.span_hint: section: Prometheus as an Operating Model, paragraph after component table
claims.rationale: The Prometheus overview defines Prometheus as a monitoring and time-series metrics system.

claims.claim_text: A scrape is an HTTP request to a target endpoint, usually `/metrics`, and Prometheus attaches labels such as `job` and `instance`; failed scrapes record `up == 0`.
claims.span_hint: section: Scraping, Targets, and the Pull Model, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: The jobs and instances page documents scrape targets, auto labels, and `up` semantics.

claims.claim_text: Prometheus Kubernetes service discovery can discover Pods, Services, Endpoints, or EndpointSlices and expose Kubernetes metadata for relabeling.
claims.span_hint: section: Scraping, Targets, and the Pull Model, paragraph 2
claims.rationale: The Prometheus configuration reference documents Kubernetes service discovery roles and meta labels.

claims.claim_text: Pushgateway is intended for short-lived batch jobs and should not be the default path for long-running services.
claims.span_hint: section: Scraping, Targets, and the Pull Model, paragraph after Kubernetes object diagram
claims.rationale: The Prometheus pushing best-practices page directly states the Pushgateway use case and cautions.

claims.claim_text: ServiceMonitor configurations often select endpoints by port name rather than port number, so the Service port name matters.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Fixing Kubernetes Service Discovery, paragraph before Service YAML
claims.rationale: The Prometheus Operator API reference documents ServiceMonitor endpoints and port selection.

claims.claim_text: The example ServiceMonitor selects Services by `app.kubernetes.io/name` and scrapes the named `metrics` endpoint every fifteen seconds.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Fixing Kubernetes Service Discovery, paragraph before ServiceMonitor YAML
claims.rationale: The Operator API reference documents ServiceMonitor selectors and endpoint fields.

claims.claim_text: Many Helm installations of `kube-prometheus-stack` configure Prometheus to select monitors using a release label matching the Helm release name.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Fixing Kubernetes Service Discovery, paragraph after ServiceMonitor YAML
claims.rationale: The kube-prometheus-stack chart documentation describes release-label-based monitor selection behavior.

claims.claim_text: Raw Prometheus `kubernetes_sd_configs` expose Kubernetes metadata as labels beginning with `__meta_kubernetes_`.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Fixing Kubernetes Service Discovery, paragraph before scrape_configs YAML
claims.rationale: The Prometheus configuration reference documents Kubernetes discovery meta labels.

claims.claim_text: `rate()` handles counter resets and estimates the average per-second increase over a selected time range.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Building PromQL From a Symptom, step three
claims.rationale: The PromQL functions documentation defines `rate()` behavior for counters.

claims.claim_text: Prometheus histograms store cumulative bucket counters, and `histogram_quantile()` requires the `le` bucket-boundary label to remain in the aggregation.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Building PromQL From a Symptom, latency histogram paragraph
claims.rationale: The PromQL function reference documents histogram buckets and `histogram_quantile()` requirements.

claims.claim_text: `rate()` is better suited to alerts and SLOs, while `irate()` uses the last two samples and is better for short debugging sessions.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Building PromQL From a Symptom, paragraph before RATE AND IRATE diagram
claims.rationale: The PromQL functions documentation distinguishes `rate()` and `irate()` in these terms.

claims.claim_text: Recording rules evaluate expressions ahead of time and store the result as new time series for reuse and faster queries.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Building PromQL From a Symptom, paragraph before recording rules YAML
claims.rationale: Prometheus recording-rules guidance documents precomputing reused or expensive expressions.

claims.claim_text: Alertmanager groups, deduplicates, silences, and routes alerts by labels.
claims.span_hint: section: Alerting Rules, Recording Rules, and Human Action, paragraph before Alertmanager YAML
claims.rationale: The Alertmanager documentation directly describes grouping, inhibition/silencing, deduplication, and routing responsibilities.

claims.claim_text: Prometheus stores one time series for each unique metric name and label set, so cardinality can dominate scale more than raw traffic volume.
claims.span_hint: section: Storage, Cardinality, and Production Scale, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: The Prometheus data model and naming guidance support the series-identity and label-cardinality claims.

claims.claim_text: Prometheus scrape configuration can drop labels and enforce sample and label limits on scrape jobs.
claims.span_hint: section: Storage, Cardinality, and Production Scale, paragraph before scrape limit YAML
claims.rationale: The Prometheus configuration reference documents metric relabeling and scrape limit fields.

claims.claim_text: Remote write streams samples from Prometheus to another system.
claims.span_hint: section: Storage, Cardinality, and Production Scale, paragraph before remote_write YAML
claims.rationale: The Prometheus configuration reference directly documents `remote_write`; the proposed anchor gives the exact section.

claims.claim_text: Federation lets one Prometheus scrape selected time series from another Prometheus, while remote write streams samples to a storage backend.
claims.span_hint: section: Storage, Cardinality, and Production Scale, paragraph after remote_write YAML
claims.rationale: The Prometheus federation documentation defines federation as scraping selected series from another server.

claims.claim_text: The RED pattern means rate, errors, and duration for request-driven services.
claims.span_hint: section: Dashboards, SLOs, and Query Review, paragraph 2
claims.rationale: Grafana's RED Method article is an allowlisted source that directly defines the named pattern.

claims.claim_text: The USE pattern means utilization, saturation, and errors for infrastructure resources.
claims.span_hint: section: Dashboards, SLOs, and Query Review, paragraph 3
claims.rationale: The primary source for the USE Method is off the trusted-domain allowlist, so it needs allowlist review.

claims.claim_text: Prometheus querying guidance warns that broad or frequent queries can overload the server and recommends recording expensive expressions.
claims.span_hint: section: Dashboards, SLOs, and Query Review, paragraph after signal pattern table
claims.rationale: The querying basics page warns about expensive queries, and the rules page supports pre-recording expensive expressions.

claims.claim_text: Prometheus was designed to stay useful during partial failure, with local scraping and local alert evaluation central to the design.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know?, item 1
claims.rationale: The Prometheus overview discusses its operational model, local storage, and alert generation design.

claims.claim_text: The `up` metric is generated by Prometheus rather than by the target.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know?, item 2
claims.rationale: The jobs and instances page defines `up` as generated from scrape success.

claims.claim_text: Histogram buckets are cumulative in Prometheus.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know?, item 3
claims.rationale: The PromQL function reference documents histogram bucket behavior used by `histogram_quantile()`.

claims.claim_text: EndpointSlices represent network endpoints behind a Service, including endpoint addresses and conditions.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Fixing Kubernetes Service Discovery, opening paragraph and exercise verification steps
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes EndpointSlice documentation directly supports the EndpointSlice discovery behavior described in the module.

claims.claim_text: The `kube-prometheus-stack` Helm chart installs Prometheus Operator support, Prometheus, Alertmanager, exporters, and custom resources used for ServiceMonitor discovery.
claims.span_hint: section: Hands-On Exercise, Step 1 paragraph
claims.rationale: The chart README documents the stack components and CRD/operator-based resource model.

further_reading.title: Prometheus Overview
further_reading.why_relevant: Canonical starting point for Prometheus architecture, pull scraping, local storage, service discovery, and alerting.

further_reading.title: Prometheus Configuration Reference
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for Kubernetes service discovery, relabeling, scrape limits, and remote write configuration.

further_reading.title: PromQL Functions
further_reading.why_relevant: Defines `rate()`, `irate()`, histogram functions, and counter handling used throughout the module.

further_reading.title: Alertmanager Documentation
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains alert grouping, routing, deduplication, and silencing concepts used in the alerting section.

further_reading.title: Prometheus Operator API Reference
further_reading.why_relevant: Documents ServiceMonitor and Prometheus custom resource fields used in the Kubernetes discovery examples.

notes: Source IDs S001-S014 refer to the module's existing Sources list in order. Newly proposed allowlisted URLs are included only where the module source list does not directly cover the claim.
